title: Spec-first or issue-first
body: Start from local proposal.md/tasks.md or pull an existing issue tracker issue with specsync pull.

title: Planning scan
body: specsync scan shows what already exists for an area — in-flight changes, loose issues, recent commits — before you plan.

title: Idempotent
body: Run specsync -change my-change as often as needed. If the issue tracker issue already exists, specsync updates it (same identity marker) instead of creating a duplicate.

title: Workflow state management
body: Set stage explicitly with specsync set-stage my-change [active|blocked|in-review|complete] so progress is visible and committed in metadata.

title: Priority-driven dispatch
body: Set priorities (1-100) with specsync set-priority my-change 85. Agents respect priority when deciding what to work on next — humans direct focus.

title: Projects board sync
body: Project and sync with specsync -change my-change -project my-org/6. Add -status-map "active=In Progress,complete=Done" to align board columns with change stages.

title: Release follow-up
body: specsync release-plan highlights shipped changes, loose/unlinked work, archive candidates, and advisory release impact from spec-level deltas.

title: Spec-driven changelog
body: specsync changelog writes a Keep a Changelog section from your shipped changes — one entry per change, release notes authored at planning time, never a raw commit dump.
